title: Gonalston Lancaster L7578 Memorial
cause_of_loss: Suspected parachute harness entangled in controls; aircraft dived from 8,000 ft
narrative: Lancaster L7578 was historically significant — originally built as a Manchester bomber in 1940, later converted to a Lancaster. It had served with Squadrons 97, 207, 83, and multiple Conversion Units. Notably, it was the aircraft used by Wing Commander John Nettleton VC for training prior to the famous Augsburg Raid. On 26 May 1944, P/O Sanderson's crew took off for a training exercise. The aircraft climbed to 8,000 feet, then suddenly dived into the ground at Gonalston. One theory suggests a parachute harness became entangled in the controls. All seven were buried together in a collective grave at Newark Cemetery.

crew.name: George Benjamin Sanderson
crew.burial_location: Newark Cemetery, Sec F, Collective Grave 309
